steps.title: Identify the core requirement
steps.content: To compare the curviness of two rivers with different sinuosity levels, the answer must directly state which river has the greater ratio of stream length to valley length, not merely define sinuosity or explain how it's calculated.

steps.title: Apply that requirement to find the answer
steps.content:
Choice A — The Willapa River's ratio of stream length to valley length, also known as sinuosity, is greater than that of the Neales River. directly compares the Willapa River's stream-to-valley ratio to the Neales River's, establishing that Willapa's is greater.

Choices B and D explain how sinuosity is calculated without comparing the two specific rivers, and Choice C — The high-sinuosity Neales River in Australia is curvier than the low-sinuosity Willapa River in the United States. contradicts the notes by labeling the rivers' sinuosity levels backward.

That matches Choice A — The Willapa River's ratio of stream length to valley length, also known as sinuosity, is greater than that of the Neales River..
